explanation:
## Understanding Thermal Requirements

Building and renovation require precise technical calculations: concrete strength, steel reinforcement, bricks, plaster, waterproofing. IRAM (Instituto Argentino de Normalización y Metrología) and related standards establish these processes.

## How It Works

Thermal transmittance (K-value) indicates how much heat passes through one square meter of material per degree Celsius of temperature difference. IRAM 11605 specifies minimum K values based on your climate zone to ensure energy efficiency and building comfort.

## Important Notes

This calculation is an orientation reference. For critical decisions (construction permits, energy certifications, legal compliance), consult with a licensed engineer or building professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is K-value in building insulation?
faq.a: K-value (thermal transmittance) measures how much heat passes through material per square meter per degree Celsius. Lower K = better insulation performance.
Argentina's technical standard specifying minimum thermal transmittance values by climate zone, ensuring energy efficiency and building comfort.

faq.q: How do I know my climate zone?
faq.a: Argentina divides into 6 zones: I-II (warm/coastal), III (temperate central), IV-V (cold south), VI (very cold mountains).

faq.q: What's the difference between K=0.5 and K=1.0?
faq.a: K=0.5 allows half the heat transfer of K=1.0, meaning 2× better insulation. Lower K values indicate superior thermal performance.

faq.q: How can I achieve lower K-values?
faq.a: Add insulation material: 5cm polystyrene (~K=0.6), 10cm fiberglass (~K=0.4), or mineral wool for superior performance.

faq.q: Is thermal insulation required by law?
faq.a: In Buenos Aires (CABA), it's mandatory for new construction. Nationwide, not legally required but recommended for energy efficiency.

faq.q: Can I trust these calculation results?
faq.a: Yes, we use internationally validated formulas. Always consult a licensed engineer for critical construction decisions.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free and require no registration or login.

faq.a: No. All calculations run in your browser. We never send your data to external servers.
